name: npm-badges-buried
query: create a client and send a request
readme:
# acme

![build](https://img.shields.io/badge/build.svg) ![cov](https://img.shields.io/badge/cov.svg)

## Table of Contents
- [Install](#install)
- [Usage](#usage)

## Installation
```sh
npm i acme
```

## Background
Long history paragraph about motivation, prior art, design philosophy, goals and non-goals, and roadmap notes that go on for a while to push usage past a small budget.

More architecture prose, acknowledgements, and comparisons with alternatives, continuing for several lines so the usage section is well past the top.

## Usage
```ts
import { AcmeClient } from 'acme';
const client = new AcmeClient({ apiKey: 'x' });
await client.send('/path', { body: {} });
```

## License
MIT

name: npm-api-reference
readme:
# parseutil

## Installation
```sh
npm i parseutil
```

## parse(input)
Parses a string.
```ts
parse('a,b');
```

## parseOptions(input, opts)
Parse with options.
```ts
parseOptions('a;b', { sep: ';' });
```

## stringify(value)
Inverse of parse.
```ts
stringify(['a','b']);
```

## License
MIT

name: python-usage
readme:
# pipeflow

## Installation
```sh
pip install pipeflow
```

## About
A data pipeline library with a long blurb about design, history, and rationale spanning a couple of lines to push usage down.

Even more about ecosystem fit and comparisons and philosophy for padding here.

## Usage
```python
from pipeflow import Pipeline
p = Pipeline()
p.run(steps)
```

## License
MIT

name: rust-usage
query: spawn a task on the pool
readme:
# taskpool

## Installation
```sh
cargo add taskpool
```

## Overview
A tiny task pool. Background and motivation text that runs on for a bit to push the usage example below a small token budget so prefix truncation would miss it entirely.

## Usage
```rust
use taskpool::Pool;
let pool = Pool::new(4);
pool.spawn(|| work());
```

## License
MIT

# blobly

A small utility library, installed via npm i blobly, with no section headings at all.

Some background prose about why it exists and what problems it solves, padded to be reasonably long so the usage is not at the very top of the file.

More filler about the philosophy and comparisons and trade-offs to push things down further still.

```ts
import { blob } from 'blobly';
blob.pack(data);
```

Trailing notes about license (MIT) and contributing guidelines and links to the docs site.
